message:
feat(core,parser,layout,render-svg,render-term): add sequence notes, fragments, theme config, and dot parser improvements

Sequence rendering: notes (yellow boxes), fragments (loop/alt/par/opt/
critical/break with dashed borders), DottedCross arrow type.
Parser: fragment/note parsing, dot parser improvements.
Layout: lifecycle marker refinements.
Rendering: theme.rs config, canvas context, terminal parity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-0bu2","title":"Implement IrGanttMeta for gantt chart date-aware features","description":"# Gantt Chart IR Metadata

## Purpose
Gantt charts need date-aware metadata that the generic IR doesn't support. This task adds gantt-specific metadata structures.

## Types to Add
1. IrGanttMeta { date_format: String, title: Option<String>, excludes: Vec<GanttExclude>, sections: Vec<IrGanttSection> }
2. IrGanttSection { name: String, tasks: Vec<IrGanttTask> }
3. IrGanttTask { id: String, name: String, task_type: GanttTaskType, start: GanttDate, end: GanttDate, depends_on: Vec<String>, progress: Option<f32> }
4. GanttTaskType enum: Normal, Critical, Done, Active, Milestone
5. GanttDate enum: Absolute(String), AfterTask(String), Duration(String)
6. GanttExclude enum: Weekends, Dates(Vec<String>)
7. Add gantt_meta: Option<IrGanttMeta> to MermaidDiagramIr

## This unblocks: gantt date parsing and gantt SVG rendering tasks","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:41.257934926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31.832582133Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fm-core","gantt","infrastructure","parity"],"comments":[{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-0bu2","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"},{"id":197,"issue_id":"bd-0bu2","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL FIELDS NEEDED in IrGanttMeta:
- tick_interval: Option<GanttTickInterval> (Day, Week, Month, Quarter, Year)
- axis_format: Option<String> (strftime format for axis labels)  
- inclusive_end_dates: bool
- today_marker_style: Option<String> (CSS-like style string)
- weekday_start: Option<u8> (0=Sunday through 6=Saturday)

These are required for full gantt parity.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-0n3y","title":"Sequence: support autonumber start and increment parity","description":"# Sequence autonumber parameter parity

Mermaid sequence diagrams support more than bare `autonumber`; they also support explicit numeric parameters such as `autonumber 10` and `autonumber 10 5`.

Scope
- Parse `autonumber <start>` and `autonumber <start> <increment>` in sequence diagrams
- Store the starting number and increment in sequence IR metadata
- Render numbered sequence message labels using the configured progression instead of always starting at 1 with step 1
- Preserve existing bare `autonumber` behavior as start=1 increment=1
- Add parser/core/render regression coverage

Acceptance Criteria
- No unsupported-syntax warning for parameterized `autonumber` lines
- IR round-trips the configured numbering parameters cleanly
- SVG numbering reflects configured start and increment deterministically
- Focused tests plus relevant workspace quality gates pass via `rch` for cargo operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:16.938161393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:36:17.108872718Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T20:36:17.108376919Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence autonumber parameter parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-0pi0","title":"Packet-Beta: Implement bit numbering and field type rendering","description":"# Packet-Beta Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Bit numbering along top/bottom of packet header not rendered
- Field type/description text not rendered within fields
- Byte alignment grid not rendered
- Field styling by type

## Implementation Plan
1. Render bit numbering as small labels along the top edge of the packet
2. Show field name and optional type centered within each field box
3. Draw byte alignment markers at 8-bit boundaries
4. Apply distinct fill colors for different field types if categorized","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:11.697546308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.100084908Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["packet-beta","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":167,"issue_id":"bd-0pi0","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-106","title":"EPIC: Complete Parser for All Mermaid Diagram Types","description":"Expand fm-parser to support ALL 24+ mermaid diagram types that mermaid-js supports. The existing FrankenTUI parser handles ~8 types (flowchart, sequence, class, state, gantt, pie, quadrant, packet). We need to add: ER diagram, git graph, mindmap, timeline, user journey, sankey, C4, kanban, architecture, block diagram, radar chart, XY chart, treemap, requirement diagram, plus the DOT format bridge. Each new diagram type needs: tokenizer rules, AST types in fm-core, parser producing MermaidDiagramIr, and test fixtures. Additionally, implement graceful error recovery so malformed/corrupted input degrades to best-effort rendering rather than failing. This is where we differentiate from mermaid-js by being SMARTER about user intent.

## Success Criteria

- Every capability listed in this epic is delivered completely through child issues; no scope cuts and no loss of planned functionality.
- Child tasks include comprehensive unit/integration coverage and e2e coverage with detailed logging; test evidence is available and reproducible.
- Performance, determinism, and reliability expectations for this epic are validated with measurable checks.
- User-facing behavior is documented (README/docs/config/migration notes) for all newly shipped functionality.
- Release readiness is proven by passing workspace quality gates and epic-level validation flows end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:30:01.329811699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:04:33.693357514Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:04:33.692624085Z","close_reason":"All 24 Mermaid diagram type parsers are implemented: flowchart, sequence, class, state, ER, gitgraph, gantt, pie, mindmap, timeline, journey, quadrant, sankey, C4 (5 variants), architecture, block-beta, packet, kanban, xychart, requirement. Detection with fuzzy matching and content heuristics also complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-106","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-106.1","title":"Add ER (Entity-Relationship) diagram parser","description":"Implement parser for ER diagrams. Syntax: entities with attributes (PK, FK, type, name), relationships with cardinality (||--o{, }|..|{, etc.). Map to IR: entities become IrNodes with members (attribute list), relationships become IrEdges with labels showing cardinality. Reference: mermaid-js erDiagram syntax. Support: entity names, attribute types, relationship labels, identifying/non-identifying relationships. Test with: simple 2-entity, complex schema with 10+ entities, self-referencing tables, composite keys.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:30:22.512816771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T21:48:38.787271920Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T21:48:38.787246472Z","close_reason":"Implemented ER diagram parser with full attribute support: PK/FK/UK keys, data types, comments. Added IrEntityAttribute and IrAttributeKey to fm-core. Added 4 new comprehensive tests. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagram-type","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-106.1","depends_on_id":"bd-106","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-106.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2nw.7","title":"Implement comprehensive determinism verification harness","description":"# Determinism Verification Harness (Parity Extension)

Goal:
- Prove deterministic behavior against both internal invariants and reference parity expectations.

Scope:
- Build parity drift classification and tolerance policies on top of shared proof harnesses.
- Produce cross platform determinism evidence packages for CI and release gates.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for hash normalization and drift categorization logic.
- Integration tests for repeated parse, layout, render hash stability.
- E2E corpus replay across platforms with deterministic seeds.
- Structured logs must include run id, input hash, platform, stage hashes, drift class, tolerance rationale, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:57.189064124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:31.479806803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.353827912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.254202835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":100,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Property-Based Tests (proptest)
1. Parse-layout-render roundtrip produces identical output
2. Re-running layout with same IR = same coordinates
3. Rendering same layout = same pixel/character output
4. Hash of output is stable across runs

### Determinism Proof Infrastructure
1. Golden test corpus with checksums
2. CI job that runs 100x and asserts identical output
3. Cross-platform determinism (Linux/macOS/WASM)

### Invariant Tests
1. Layout coordinates are always finite and bounded
2. No overlapping node bounding boxes (unless intentional)
3. Edge routes don't cross node interiors
4. Cluster boundaries contain all member nodes

### Replay Tests
1. Capture full trace of layout computation
2. Replay from trace produces identical result
3. Binary diffable trace format for debugging

### Logging Requirements
- JSON trace of all layout decisions
- Hash checkpoint at each pipeline stage
- Structured diff when non-determinism detected","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.8","title":"Build profile-first performance optimization suite with behavior proofs","description":"# Profile First Performance Optimization Suite (Parity Extension)

Goal:
- Compare performance against reference and drive optimization prioritization with behavior proof guarantees.

Scope:
- Reuse shared baseline and optimization waves from bd-3uz.
- Add parity focused performance deltas and hotspot attribution versus reference.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for benchmark ingestion, normalization, and scoring.
- Integration tests for reproducible benchmark pipeline execution.
- E2E performance sweeps on representative and adversarial corpus segments.
- Structured logs must include p50 p95 p99 latency, throughput, memory proxy, hotspot ids, proof artifact links, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:17:14.500306875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:31.636668099Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","performance","profiling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.610772627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.468586936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.580881435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.696892032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":101,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Profiling Infrastructure
1. Criterion benchmarks for parse/layout/render hot paths
2. CPU flame graph generation automation
3. Memory allocation profiling with dhat
4. WASM-specific profiling with browser DevTools

### Behavior Proof Requirements
For each optimization:
1. Before/after benchmark with statistical significance
2. Property test proving semantic equivalence
3. Determinism test proving output unchanged
4. Documentation of change and measured impact

### Regression Detection
1. Criterion historical comparison
2. Alert on >5% regression in any benchmark
3. CI gate on performance budgets

### E2E Performance Validation
```bash
# scripts/perf-validation.sh
hyperfine --warmup 3 'fm-cli render corpus/large.mmd'
# Assert p95 latency < 500ms for large diagrams
```

### Logging Requirements
- Structured timing data per stage
- Memory high-water mark tracking
- Hot path entry/exit tracing (compile-time optional)","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.9","title":"Create comprehensive E2E validation pipeline with structured logging","description":"# Comprehensive E2E Validation Pipeline (Parity Extension)

Goal:
- Build end to end parity validation by composing shared CLI and WASM stress pipelines with reference comparison logic.

Scope:
- Reuse bd-3uz.22, bd-3uz.23, and bd-3uz.24 outputs and schemas.
- Add parity dimensions including semantic diff classes and visual diff signals where applicable.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for comparator and report rollup logic.
- Integration tests for ingesting CLI and WASM telemetry into parity reports.
- E2E runs over normal, malformed recoverable, and adversarial corpus fixtures.
- Structured logs must include reference hash, FM hash, semantic diff class, visual diff score, timings, diagnostics, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:17:35.256287916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:31.792893935Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","testing","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.397315569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.307477234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.815323472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.942479258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.063898001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.24","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.185815459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":102,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### E2E Test Scripts
```bash
# scripts/e2e-validation.sh

# Test all diagram types parse correctly
for type in flowchart sequence class state er gantt pie gitgraph; do
  fm-cli parse --validate corpus/${type}/*.mmd
done

# Test full pipeline
fm-cli render corpus/all/*.mmd --output=test_output/
diff -r test_output/ golden_output/

# Test error cases
fm-cli parse corpus/invalid/*.mmd --expected-errors
```

### Logging Infrastructure
1. Structured JSON logs at each pipeline stage
2. Log schema validation (jq-parseable)
3. Log aggregation for multi-file runs
4. Trace ID correlation across stages

### Coverage Requirements
1. Line coverage >90% for core modules
2. Branch coverage >80%
3. Integration test coverage for all CLI commands
4. Error path coverage for all recoverable errors

### CI Integration
1. E2E suite runs on every PR
2. Golden file updates require review
3. Log schema changes trigger alerts","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:06Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2puw","title":"Sequence: parse participant stereotype/config declarations and preserve actor glyph shape intent","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T19:58:31.951426301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:05:44.613586020Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T20:05:44.613091624Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","sequence"]}
 {"id":"bd-2q3f","title":"EPIC: Geometric/Conformal Algebra for Transforms (§12.11)","description":"## Geometric/Conformal Algebra for Transforms (§12.11)

Replace the conventional matrix-based coordinate transform pipeline (translation, rotation, scaling, reflection) with Conformal Geometric Algebra (CGA), unifying all rigid-body and conformal transformations under a single algebraic framework. This enables elegant composition of transforms, intersection queries, and constraint solving using the same multivector operations.

## Motivation
FrankenMermaid's rendering pipeline applies many coordinate transforms: viewport mapping, zoom/pan, node placement, edge routing with Bezier curves, text positioning, and SVG viewBox calculations. Currently these use a mix of 2D affine matrices and ad-hoc vector arithmetic. CGA unifies ALL of these into a single algebra where:
- Points, lines, circles, and planes are all represented as multivectors
- Translation, rotation, and scaling are all rotors (geometric products of vectors)
- Intersection of any two geometric objects is a single algebraic operation (meet/join)
- Transforms compose by rotor multiplication (associative, no matrix decomposition needed)

## Mathematical Foundation
Conformal Geometric Algebra embeds 2D Euclidean space into a 4D projective space via the conformal model R_{3,1}:
- Basis vectors: e1, e2 (Euclidean), e+ (positive extra), e- (negative extra)
- Null vectors: e_o = (e- - e+)/2 (origin), e_inf = e+ + e- (infinity)
- Point embedding: P(x,y) = x*e1 + y*e2 + (x^2+y^2)/2 * e_inf + e_o
- Translation rotor: T = 1 - (d/2)*e_inf where d is displacement bivector
- Rotation rotor: R = cos(t/2) + sin(t/2)*e12 where e12 = e1 wedge e2
- Scaling rotor: S = cosh(s/2) + sinh(s/2)*e+ wedge e-
- Composed transform: M = T*R*S (just multiply rotors)
- Apply to point: P' = M*P*M_rev (sandwich product, M_rev is reverse of M)

## Key Papers
- Dorst, Fontijne & Mann, "Geometric Algebra for Computer Science" (2007)
- Hildenbrand, "Foundations of Geometric Algebra Computing" (2013)
- Wareham, Cameron & Lasenby, "Applications of CGA" (2005)
- De Keninck & Dorst, "Guided Tour of GA" (2019)

## Graveyard Reference: §12.11 -- Geometric/Conformal Algebra for Transforms
Score: 2.5 (Alien uplift). Risk: learning curve, performance vs optimized matrix code.

## Success Metrics
- All coordinate transforms in rendering pipeline expressed as CGA rotors
- Transform composition 3-5 operations instead of matrix multiply (code clarity metric)
- Intersection queries (edge-node, viewport clipping) expressed as meet/join operations
- Performance: CGA transform pipeline within 2x of optimized matrix pipeline (acceptable for clarity gains)
- If performance gap > 2x: provide matrix backend with CGA API wrapper (zero-cost abstraction goal)

## Risk Mitigation
CGA's 4D multivectors have 16 components vs 6 for 2D affine matrix. For hot inner loops (rendering 10K+ nodes), this overhead may be unacceptable. Strategy:
1. Use CGA for transform composition and constraint solving (where algebraic elegance matters)
2. Extract final affine matrix from CGA rotor for batch rendering (one-time conversion)
3. SIMD-optimize the sandwich product for the hot path
4. If CGA overhead > 2x in benchmarks: keep CGA for API/constraint layer, matrix for rendering","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:37:30.048794600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:29:55.308398714Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","conformal","geometric-algebra","rendering","transforms"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q3f","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:29:55.308338220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2q3f","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:16.449530378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2q3f.1","title":"Implement CGA multivector types and rotor algebra in fm-core","description":"## CGA Multivector Types and Rotor Algebra (§12.11.1)

Implement the foundational Conformal Geometric Algebra types: multivectors in R_{3,1}, rotor construction, sandwich product, and conversion to/from conventional 2D affine matrices.

## Mathematical Foundation
In the conformal model R_{3,1} for 2D geometry:
- **Basis:** {1, e1, e2, e+, e-, e12, e1+, e1-, e2+, e2-, e+-, e12+, e12-, e1+-, e2+-, e12+-}
  That is 2^4 = 16 basis blades for a general multivector.
- **Even subalgebra (rotors):** {1, e12, e1+, e1-, e2+, e2-, e+-, e12+-} — 8 components
  Rotors are even-grade multivectors with unit norm: R*R̃ = 1.
- **Key operations:**
  - Geometric product: ab = a·b + a∧b (combines inner and outer product)
  - Sandwich product: x' = RxR̃ (applies transform R to object x)
  - Reverse: R̃ reverses the order of basis vectors in each blade
  - Norm: |R|² = R*R̃ (scalar part)

**Rotor construction helpers:**
- `Rotor::translation(dx, dy)`: T = 1 - (dx*e1 + dy*e2)*e_∞/2
- `Rotor::rotation(angle)`: R = cos(θ/2) + sin(θ/2)*e1∧e2
- `Rotor::scale(factor)`: S = cosh(ln(s)/2) + sinh(ln(s)/2)*e+∧e-
- `Rotor::compose(r1, r2)`: M = r1 * r2 (geometric product)

**Conversion to affine matrix:**
Extract the 2x3 affine matrix [a,b,tx; c,d,ty] from a CGA rotor by applying the rotor to the basis points (0,0), (1,0), (0,1) and reading off coefficients. This is the bridge to conventional rendering.

## Implementation in FrankenMermaid
1. Create `fm-core/src/cga.rs` module with `Multivector4D` type (16 f64 components, stored as [f64; 16]).
2. Implement `Rotor` type (wrapper over even-grade multivector, 8 f64 components).
3. Implement geometric product, sandwich product, reverse, norm.
4. Implement rotor constructors: translation, rotation, scale, reflection.
5. Implement `Rotor::to_affine_matrix() -> AffineMatrix2D` conversion.
6. Implement `AffineMatrix2D::to_rotor() -> Rotor` conversion (for interop).
7. SIMD optimization: use `std::simd` or `packed_simd` for the 8-component rotor multiply.
8. Comprehensive property tests: rotor composition associativity, inverse correctness, matrix round-trip.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multivector and Rotor types with full geometric product implementation
- [ ] All rotor constructors produce correct transforms (verified against matrix equivalents)
- [ ] Sandwich product correctly transforms points, lines, circles
- [ ] Rotor↔AffineMatrix round-trip error < 1e-12 (property test, 10000 random rotors)
- [ ] Rotor composition is associative to within 1e-10 (property test)
- [ ] SIMD-optimized rotor multiply benchmarked against scalar baseline
- [ ] #[no_std] compatible for WASM target","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:37:54.293509980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:37:54.293509980Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","conformal","geometric-algebra","math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q3f.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2q3f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:37:54.293509980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2q3f.2","title":"Replace rendering transform pipeline with CGA rotor composition","description":"## CGA Rotor Composition in Rendering Pipeline (§12.11.2)

Replace the current matrix-based transform stack in fm-render-svg and fm-render-term with CGA rotor composition, enabling elegant multi-stage transform pipelines.

## Mathematical Foundation
Current pipeline: SVG rendering applies transforms as matrix multiplications:
  M_final = M_viewport * M_zoom * M_pan * M_node_placement
Each M is a 3x3 homogeneous matrix (or 2x3 affine). Transform composition: 6 multiplies per matrix pair, 18 multiply-adds for 3x3.

CGA pipeline: same transforms as rotor composition:
  R_final = R_viewport * R_zoom * R_pan * R_node_placement
Each R is an 8-component even multivector. Composition: geometric product of even multivectors, ~64 multiply-adds. BUT: rotors compose more naturally (no matrix decomposition needed for extracting rotation/scale/translation), and the composed rotor can be cached and applied to many points via sandwich product.

**Key advantage:** In CGA, extracting "what rotation is embedded in this composed transform" is trivial (read off the e12 component). In matrices, this requires SVD or atan2 decomposition. This matters for text rendering (text must remain upright even when diagram is rotated).

## Implementation in FrankenMermaid
1. Define `TransformStack` type that stores a composed CGA rotor instead of a matrix stack.
2. Push/pop operations compose/decompose rotors.
3. For SVG output: convert final rotor to `transform="matrix(a,b,c,d,e,f)"` attribute.
4. For terminal output: convert final rotor to integer cell coordinates (with rounding).
5. For text rendering: extract rotation component from rotor to counter-rotate text.
6. Implement viewport clipping using CGA meet operation (line ∧ circle = intersection points).
7. Cache composed rotors per diagram region for incremental re-rendering.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TransformStack API used in both SVG and terminal renderers
- [ ] SVG output bit-identical to current matrix-based output (regression test)
- [ ] Text counter-rotation works correctly for rotated diagrams
- [ ] Viewport clipping via CGA meet operation produces correct clip paths
- [ ] Benchmark: rotor pipeline within 2x of matrix pipeline for 10K-node rendering
- [ ] Transform stack push/pop is O(1) (rotor multiply)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:38:09.355229963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:41.337378188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","conformal","geometric-algebra","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q3f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2q3f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:38:09.355229963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2q3f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2q3f.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:41.337319388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-jj29","title":"Sequence: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# Sequence Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Sequence diagrams have the LARGEST gap of any core diagram type (~60% parser). Only basic participant/actor declarations and simple messages are supported. All advanced features — which are what make sequence diagrams actually useful in practice — are missing.

## Critical Gaps
1. Activation/deactivation (lifeline active periods)
2. Notes (left of, right of, over)
3. Interaction fragments (loop, alt, opt, par, critical, break, rect)
4. Autonumber
5. Actor/participant aliases and creation/destruction
6. Message types (async, return, lost/found)
7. Box grouping of participants

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/sequenceDiagram.html
- Parser: crates/fm-parser/src/mermaid_parser.rs lines 1041-1107
- Layout: crates/fm-layout/src/lib.rs (layout_diagram_sequence_traced)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.308246204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:44.990262997Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jj29","depends_on_id":"bd-508m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:44.181969467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jj29","depends_on_id":"bd-7g6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:42.517792492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jj29","depends_on_id":"bd-aknv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:41.710947944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jj29","depends_on_id":"bd-drav","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:43.343610442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jj29","depends_on_id":"bd-e1sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:44.989305746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-keyp","title":"Flowchart: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# Flowchart Feature Parity

## Current State
Flowchart is the most complete diagram type (~90% parser, ~85% SVG render). The core graph structure (nodes, edges, subgraphs, directions) works well. The gaps are in styling directives, interactive features, and constraint hints.

## Gaps to Close
1. classDef directive: define reusable style classes
2. style directive: apply inline styles to specific nodes
3. linkStyle directive: style specific edges
4. click directive rendering: make nodes clickable with URLs/callbacks
5. Tooltip support on hover
6. accDescription accessibility descriptions
7. Subgraph direction override (direction within subgraph)
8. Node markdown label support (using backtick syntax)

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/flowchart.html
- Parser: crates/fm-parser/src/mermaid_parser.rs lines 777-986
- SVG renderer: crates/fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:42:02.971032563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:30.830633243Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-7ib6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:40.904059282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-bfsm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:37.663093628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-bium","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:30.829700237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-peow","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:39.308730074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-xwm3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:40.108139881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-y6o8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:38.499539334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-qj2b","title":"Implement state notes, guards, and transition actions","description":"# State Notes, Guards, and Actions

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
stateDiagram-v2
  state Active
  note right of Active: This state is active
  note left of Active
    Multi-line
    note content
  end note
  Active --> Done : complete [isValid] / cleanup()
  Active : entry: startTimer()
  Active : exit: stopTimer()
\`\`\`
- Notes: positioned left/right of states
- Guard conditions: [condition] on transitions
- Actions: / action() on transitions
- Entry/exit actions: declared as state descriptions

## Current State
- Notes: NOT parsed
- Guards/actions on transitions: NOT parsed (treated as part of label text)
- Entry/exit actions: NOT parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`note right of\`/\`note left of\` statements. Parse guard conditions in brackets and actions after / in transition labels. Parse \`StateName : entry: action\` / \`exit: action\`.
2. **IR**: Add notes, guard/action fields to edges, entry/exit to nodes.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Draw note boxes, show guard/action text on edges.
4. **Tests**: Notes, guarded transitions, entry/exit actions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:45:20.380064165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.874643615Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","state"],"comments":[{"id":154,"issue_id":"bd-qj2b","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-qsr8","title":"Kanban: Implement card metadata, WIP limits, and priority styling","description":"# Kanban Rendering Improvements

## What mermaid-js Supports (v11+)
\`\`\`
kanban
  Todo
    id1[Design] @{ priority: high, assigned: alice }
    id2[Implement]
  In Progress @{ wip: 2 }
    id3[Testing]
  Done
    id4[Deploy]
\`\`\`
- Card metadata: priority, assigned, tags via @{} syntax
- Column WIP limits: @{ wip: N } on column headers
- Ticket base URL for linking cards to issue trackers
- Card styling based on priority/status

## Current State
- Columns and cards parsed
- @{} metadata syntax NOT parsed
- WIP limits NOT parsed or rendered
- No priority-based styling

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse @{key: value} syntax on cards and columns. Extract priority, assigned, tags, wip.
2. **IR**: Add metadata map to kanban card nodes and WIP limit to clusters.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Style cards by priority (high=red border, medium=yellow, low=green). Show WIP limit indicator on column headers. Show assigned name as badge.
4. **Tests**: Card metadata, WIP limits, priority styling.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:12.403662639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.600150293Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["kanban","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":171,"issue_id":"bd-qsr8","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-tvxc","title":"Class: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# Class Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Class diagrams parse basic class declarations and relationships (~75% parser). The main gaps are member visibility modifiers, type annotations, generics, stereotypes, and namespace grouping.

## Gaps
1. Visibility modifiers (+, -, #, ~) on members/methods
2. Type annotations (returnType, parameterTypes)
3. Generic/template parameters (Class~T~)
4. Stereotypes (<<interface>>, <<abstract>>, <<enum>>)
5. Annotations and notes
6. Namespace grouping
7. Cardinality/multiplicity on relationship ends
8. Direction specification (class diagram direction)

 {"id":"bd-u785","title":"EPIC: Killer Demo — Layout Regression Test Harness","description":"## Killer Demo — Layout Regression Test Harness

Build an automated visual regression testing harness that renders the complete FrankenMermaid test corpus to SVG/PNG, compares against golden reference images using perceptual diff, and produces a browsable HTML report. This serves dual purpose: regression detection AND an impressive demo of FrankenMermaid's rendering capabilities.

## Motivation
Layout algorithms are notoriously difficult to regression-test with unit tests alone. A pixel-level change in coordinate assignment can propagate to produce visually different (but structurally correct) layouts. Conversely, a bug might produce structurally incorrect output that looks "close enough" to pass manual review. The regression harness provides:
1. **Automated comparison:** perceptual diff between current and golden renders
2. **Browsable report:** HTML gallery showing before/after/diff for each test case
3. **Demo showcase:** the report itself IS the demo — browse all diagram types, see FrankenMermaid's quality
4. **Regression budget:** configure acceptable diff threshold (e.g., 0.1% pixel difference) per diagram type

## Perceptual Diff Algorithm
Use structural similarity index (SSIM) or perceptual hash (pHash) rather than pixel-exact comparison:
- **SSIM:** Compares luminance, contrast, and structure. Score from 0 to 1, where 1 = identical.
  SSIM(x,y) = [l(x,y)]^α * [c(x,y)]^β * [s(x,y)]^γ
  where l = luminance, c = contrast, s = structure comparison
- **pHash:** DCT-based perceptual hash. Hamming distance between hashes measures visual similarity.
- **Threshold:** SSIM > 0.99 = no regression, 0.95-0.99 = warning, < 0.95 = regression

## Implementation
1. Create `regression-harness/` binary crate in workspace.
2. Input: directory of .mmd files (test corpus) + golden reference renders.
3. Pipeline per file: parse → layout → render to SVG → rasterize to PNG (via resvg or similar).
4. Compare each PNG against golden reference using SSIM.
5. Generate HTML report: thumbnail grid, click to expand, before/after/diff overlay.
6. Golden reference update: `cargo run --bin regression-harness -- --update-goldens`.
7. CI integration: run on every PR that touches fm-layout or fm-render, fail if any SSIM < threshold.
8. Include timing data per render in the report (performance regression detection).

## Demo Aspect
The regression report doubles as the project's visual showcase:
- Categories: flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, gantt charts, etc.
- Each category shows 5-10 representative diagrams at various complexities.
- Report includes metadata: node count, edge count, layout time, render time.
- Hosted version can be published to frankenmermaid.com/gallery for public demo.

## Success Metrics
- All diagram types in test corpus rendered and compared automatically
- SSIM threshold tuned to catch real regressions while ignoring sub-pixel jitter
- HTML report loads in < 2 seconds with 500+ thumbnails
- Golden update workflow is one command + explicit commit
- CI gate blocks merge on regression (SSIM < 0.95)
- Report generation completes in < 5 minutes for full corpus

## Dependencies
- Requires fm-render-svg (bd-1y5) for SVG output
- Requires test corpus (bd-2xl.1 golden test infrastructure)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:39.374613334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:18.947294342Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","layout","regression","testing","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:18.947218691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:44.324472620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:44.427880609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
